content.claim: As of May 2026, the Hungarian government reportedly plans a structural overhaul (not abolition) of the Klebelsberg Center and school-district system to restore school-director autonomy and municipal/local community input, citing daily operational dysfunction (e.g., inability to hire substitute teachers or approve lightbulb replacement without district permission).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Plans described as 'reportedly' planned, not yet enacted
Rationale (operational dysfunction) based on spokesperson claim, not systematic audit
Competing version: Budapest District XVIII petitioned to reclaim 24 schools (2026), suggesting parallel municipal reassertion
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
